description: Review flashcards on General Features of the Cardiovascular System

cards.text: What are the three types of capillaries?
cards.answer: Continuous (muscle, brain), Fenestrated (kidney, intestine), Sinusoidal (liver, spleen, bone marrow)

tip.tip: Think about where exchange needs to be most or least restricted

cards.text: What is an end artery? Give two examples.
cards.answer: An artery with no anastomoses — blockage causes infarction. Examples: central artery of retina, cerebral arteries

tip.tip: Where does blockage cause irreversible tissue death?

cards.text: What is the Windkessel function of elastic arteries?
cards.answer: Elastic arteries stretch during systole and recoil during diastole, converting pulsatile flow to continuous flow and maintaining diastolic pressure

tip.tip: Think of the aorta as an elastic reservoir

cards.answer: Paired veins flanking an artery in the limbs — arterial pulsation aids venous return

cards.text: Define a portal system and give two examples.
cards.answer: A venous system beginning and ending in capillaries, with no intervening heart. Examples: hepatic portal system, hypothalamo-hypophyseal portal system

cards.text: What are the three branches of the arch of aorta (right to left)?
cards.answer: Brachiocephalic trunk, left common carotid artery, left subclavian artery

cards.text: What changes occur at birth in the fetal circulation?
cards.answer: Ductus arteriosus closes (becomes ligamentum arteriosum), foramen ovale closes (becomes fossa ovalis), umbilical vessels obliterate

tip.tip: Shunts close as lungs begin functioning

cards.answer: Alternative blood supply through anastomotic channels that can enlarge when primary vessels are blocked. Example: around the scapula after subclavian ligation

cards.text: Name the three tunics of a blood vessel wall and their main contents.
cards.answer: Tunica intima (endothelium + internal elastic lamina), tunica media (smooth muscle + elastic fibres), tunica adventitia (connective tissue + vasa vasorum + nerves)
